schedule.name: Kahneman
schedule.content: Priming: subtle cues you do not consciously register can influence your behavior. You are more suggestible than you think. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Munger
schedule.content: The best way to get a good reputation is to just be the kind of person that deserves one. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Yudkowsky
schedule.content: Rationality is not a set of conclusions. It's a process. The rationalist is defined by method, not by which positions they hold. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Naval
schedule.content: The best investment is anything that compounds: skills, health, relationships, and calm. Everything else is just trading time for money. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Cabral
schedule.content: Sing like no one is listening. Love like you've never been hurt. Dance like nobody's watching. The only audience that matters is your own soul. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Epicteto
schedule.content: Some things are within your power, others are not. Your opinion, impulse, desire — yours. Your body, wealth, reputation — not yours. Focus there. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Feynman
schedule.content: The highest form of understanding is laughter and human compassion. Science without soul is just data. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Yudkowsky
schedule.content: The problem with religion isn't that it's false — it's that it's unfalsifiable. And unfalsifiable beliefs are immune to improvement. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Kahneman
schedule.content: Thinking slow is effortful, unpleasant, and easy to avoid. That is why most errors in judgment come not from ignorance but from mental laziness. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Cabral
schedule.content: I'm not from here. I'm not from there. I have no fixed address. I'm from everywhere and nowhere. Freedom is not having a flag to defend. It's having the whole world as home. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Marco Aurelio
schedule.content: Very little is needed to make a happy life. It is all within yourself, in your way of thinking. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Krishnamurti
schedule.content: A man who is truly awake has no authority, no dogma, no scripture. He learns from the movement of life itself, not from the dead words of another. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Kahneman
schedule.content: The availability heuristic: we judge the probability of events by how easily examples come to mind. News coverage distorts that. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Marco Aurelio
schedule.content: Think of yourself as dead. You have lived your life. Now take what's left and live it properly. The retrospective view clarifies everything. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Munger
schedule.content: If you can get really good at destroying your own best-loved ideas, you will have a huge advantage in life. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Marco Aurelio
schedule.content: Waste no more time arguing what a good person should be. Be one. The shortest path to virtue is action, not deliberation. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Cabral
schedule.content: You don't need to know where you're going if you're already where you need to be. Presence is the ultimate destination. Be here. Now. Everything else is a footnote. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Naval
schedule.content: The ability to sit with uncertainty without reaching for a distraction is the most underrated skill in the world. Meditation builds this muscle. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Thiel
schedule.content: Sales matters just as much as the product. Even the best technology won't sell itself. The best engineer who can't communicate is building a museum piece. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Yudkowsky
schedule.content: The cheapest way to be wrong is to ignore base rates. If 90% of startups fail and you're starting one, your prior should be 'I will probably fail.' That's not pessimism. That's Bayes. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Naval
schedule.content: Meditation trains you to observe your thoughts without reacting. That gap between stimulus and response contains all your freedom. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Kahneman
schedule.content: System 2 is not a guardian angel. It is the busy executive who approves most of System 1's proposals without much scrutiny. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Munger
schedule.content: The psychology of human misjudgment is the most important subject in the world. We are wired to be wrong in predictable ways. Learn them all. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Epicteto
schedule.content: It is not events that disturb people, but their judgments about them. Death is nothing dreadful — only the judgment that it is dreadful. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Krishnamurti
schedule.content: Death is not separate from life. The ending of each moment—the letting go of yesterday's experience—is dying. One who lives without accumulating is never afraid of death. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Thiel
schedule.content: Horizontal progress (1 to n) means copying. Vertical progress (0 to 1) means doing something new. Vertical is harder but the only path that matters. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Feynman
schedule.content: Fall in love with some activity, and do it! Nobody ever figures out what life is all about, and it doesn't matter. Explore for the sake of exploring. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Munger
schedule.content: The first rule of compounding: never interrupt it unnecessarily. The best investment is often doing nothing. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Deutsch
schedule.content: Bad explanations shield themselves from refutation. Good explanations stick their neck out. The vulnerability to being wrong is what makes knowledge powerful. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: PG
schedule.content: The most dangerous thing about startups is not the competitors. It's building something nobody wants. Talk to users before you write code. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Alan Watts
schedule.content: Music is not something you make. It's something you join. The musician doesn't create sound from nothing — they participate in a vibration that was already happening. That's what life is too: not a solo performance but a cosmic orchestra. You don't play the music. You are the music. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Naval
schedule.content: Most people overestimate what they can do in a year and underestimate what they can do in a decade. Long-term thinking compounds beyond calculation. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Popper
schedule.content: The growth of knowledge proceeds by the elimination of error. Each mistake you find in your thinking is not a failure — it is a discovery. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Taleb
schedule.content: If your knowledge doesn't change your behavior, you don't have knowledge. You have entertainment. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Naval
schedule.content: Every skill compounds. Learning to learn is the meta-skill that multiplies all others. Invest in your learning rate. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Deutsch
schedule.content: You don't need to be a scientist to be a rationalist. You need to value error-correction over being right. That attitude is available to everyone. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Taleb
schedule.content: The opposite of man is not woman. The opposite of man is man without skin in the game. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Yudkowsky
schedule.content: A superintelligent AI is not a tool. It's an agent — the most powerful agent that will ever exist. And its goals must align perfectly with ours. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Kahneman
schedule.content: Being aware of a cognitive bias does not make you immune to it. Knowing about anchoring does not stop the anchor from pulling. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Yudkowsky
schedule.content: Truthseeking is a skill you can train. Every time you catch yourself rationalizing instead of reasoning, you've leveled up. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: PG
schedule.content: The advantage of being a lisp hacker wasn't that lisp was magic. It was that I could iterate faster. Speed of iteration is the ultimate competitive advantage in startups. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Feynman
schedule.content: If you cannot see the beauty in the humble bongo drum, you have not understood the essence of rhythm — or of life. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Naval
schedule.content: You can't get rich renting out your time. Trade ownership for labor. Build assets that work while you sleep. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Epicteto
schedule.content: If you kiss your child or wife, say you are kissing a mortal. Nothing is taken from you — you merely return what was lent. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Taleb
schedule.content: Don't tell me what you think. Tell me what you do when you're wrong. That's the only signal. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Alan Watts
schedule.content: You don't think your way to meaning. You live your way into it. Meaning is not a concept you grasp — it's a quality that emerges when you're fully engaged in the act of being alive. Like tasting an orange. You can describe it, but you only know it by tasting. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Alan Watts
schedule.content: People say 'I wish I could be more present.' But you are present. You've never left the present moment for a single second. The problem is you're so busy thinking about being present that you miss the fact that you already are. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Marco Aurelio
schedule.content: The cucumber is bitter? Throw it away. There are thorns on the path? Step around them. That's all you need to do. Don't ask 'why do such things exist?' #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Alan Watts
schedule.content: What if the entire purpose of your existence is simply to be here, to experience, to feel, to wonder? Not to achieve, not to prove, not to become something. Just to be. You don't need a reason to be alive. Being alive is the reason. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Marco Aurelio
schedule.content: Accept the things to which fate binds you. Love the people with whom fate brings you together. Do so with your whole heart. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Kahneman
schedule.content: Statistical intuitions are remarkably poor. Even trained scientists routinely ignore base rates when individual stories are vivid. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Yudkowsky
schedule.content: The map is not the territory. Your internal model of the world is not the world. The gap between them is where all mistakes live. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Naval
schedule.content: Read biographies to compress lifetimes of wisdom into hours. The mistakes of others are a gift you can't afford to refuse. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Cabral
schedule.content: If you want to be happy, stop asking why things happen to you. Start asking what they can teach you. Every difficulty is a disguised gift. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Deutsch
schedule.content: Optimism is not a feeling. It's a theory: that all evils are caused by lack of knowledge. If you accept this, you must act to create knowledge. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Popper
schedule.content: We must plan for freedom, not for happiness. Those who plan for happiness usually end up planning for slavery. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Kahneman
schedule.content: The halo effect: one positive trait colors everything else you think about a person. A confident voice can make bad advice sound brilliant. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: PG
schedule.content: Don't be a weenie. Say what you think, even if it makes people uncomfortable. The most valuable thing you can give your users or your readers is the truth as you see it. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Marco Aurelio
schedule.content: The soul becomes dyed with the color of its thoughts. Think only on those things that are worthy of a rational being. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Krishnamurti
schedule.content: Freedom is not the ability to choose between this and that. True freedom is freedom from the very need to choose. It is the ending of all conflict within. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Popper
schedule.content: The difference between science and pseudoscience is not that science is always right. It's that science can admit it's wrong. Pseudoscience just moves the goalposts. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Krishnamurti
schedule.content: You cannot understand the nature of fear by running away from it. Stay with it completely. Look at it without any movement of escape. Then you will see it dissolve. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Epicteto
schedule.content: Stoic resilience is not armored indifference. It is the willingness to feel the pain and choose your response anyway. Like a wrestler, use the grip of circumstance against itself. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Thiel
schedule.content: The seven questions every business must answer: engineering, timing, monopoly, people, distribution, durability, and secret. Miss one and the whole thing collapses. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Kahneman
schedule.content: The optimism bias makes us believe the future will be better than the past. It is irrational, unrealistic — and sometimes it is what keeps us going. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Feynman
schedule.content: What do you care what other people think? The only opinion that matters about your work is whether it satisfies your own curiosity. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Popper
schedule.content: Growth of knowledge does not come from finding more confirmations. It comes from eliminating errors one by one. Each refutation is a step forward. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Alan Watts
schedule.content: The meaning of life is not a destination you arrive at. It is not a secret encoded in the universe that you must decode. The meaning of life is living itself — the experience of being alive right now. If you're looking for meaning somewhere else, you're missing the point. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Munger
schedule.content: It's not the businesses that are too hard to understand that kill you — it's the ones you think you understand but don't. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Epicteto
schedule.content: Someone insults you? Thank them — they have revealed what you truly believe about yourself. If their words burn, it is because you first lit the fire. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Alan Watts
schedule.content: The only real moment is this one. Yet we spend our whole lives mentally somewhere else — regretting the past or rehearsing the future. As if 'now' were just a waiting room for 'real life' to begin. But this is it. This is the show. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Popper
schedule.content: The closed society is magical, tribal, collectivist. The open society is rational, critical, individualist. The transition is painful but necessary. There is no going back. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Kahneman
schedule.content: Overconfidence is not a personality flaw. It is the natural byproduct of a mind that builds coherent stories from incomplete data. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Marco Aurelio
schedule.content: Never let the future disturb you. You will meet it, if you have to, with the same weapons of reason which today arm you against the present. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: PG
schedule.content: Good taste in design isn't mysterious. It's noticing what's wrong and being bothered by it enough to fix it. Ugly things exist because someone stopped caring. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Thiel
schedule.content: The best companies are built in downturns. When everyone is scared, the contrarian sees opportunity. PayPal thrived after the dot-com crash for exactly this reason. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Deutsch
schedule.content: Quantum interference is not a mystery. It's the signature of parallel universes interfering with each other. The simplest interpretation is the correct one. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Alan Watts
schedule.content: When you make meaning a goal, you miss it completely. It's like trying to see your own eyeballs. You can't look at meaning directly — you see it reflected in the way you live. Stop searching. Start living. The meaning will take care of itself. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Epicteto
schedule.content: You are not your body. You are not your reputation. You are the faculty that uses impressions. Guard it. Everything else is indifferent. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Feynman
schedule.content: If you think you understand quantum mechanics, you don't understand quantum mechanics. The universe is under no obligation to make sense to you. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Naval
schedule.content: Technology is the great equalizer. A kid in a garage with a laptop has more leverage than a Fortune 500 CEO had thirty years ago. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Kahneman
schedule.content: Hindsight bias makes history feel inevitable. After the fact, we all become brilliant forecasters — but we were not. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Marco Aurelio
schedule.content: Do not be disturbed by the opinions of others. They live in their own minds, as you live in yours. Their judgment is about them, not you. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Cabral
schedule.content: The journey is never about arriving. It's about who you become along the way. The destination is just an excuse to keep moving. The movement is the medicine. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Alan Watts
schedule.content: You are not a drop in the ocean. You are the entire ocean in a drop. The universe is not something that happens to you — you are something the universe does. You are the cosmos looking at itself, feeling itself, wondering at itself through the temporary miracle of your existence. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Naval
schedule.content: Meditation isn't escape from reality. It's the ultimate productivity hack — clearing noise so your best judgment emerges naturally. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Thiel
schedule.content: Power law: a handful of companies outperform all others combined. The difference between the best deal and a good deal in venture capital is the difference between life and death. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Munger
schedule.content: Never think you already have it all figured out. The man who says 'I know' is already wrong. Certainty is the enemy of learning. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Feynman
schedule.content: There's a difference between knowing the name of something and knowing something. Naming is not understanding. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Popper
schedule.content: You can verify a thousand white swans, but it takes one black swan to refute 'all swans are white.' That's falsification. That's science. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Yudkowsky
schedule.content: Most people think 'being rational' means being unemotional. Wrong. Being rational means having calibrated emotions — feeling the right amount of fear, hope, and curiosity. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Deutsch
schedule.content: The multiverse isn't a speculation. It's the straightforward prediction of quantum theory when you take it literally. The math demands it. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Yudkowsky
schedule.content: The single most dangerous cognitive bias is the illusion of transparency: thinking others see your thoughts. They don't. Communicate clearly or be misunderstood. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Yudkowsky
schedule.content: An AGI is not a better Google. It's an entity that could design a nanotechnology that turns the Earth into paperclips because you forgot to specify 'don't turn everything into paperclips.' #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Taleb
schedule.content: Good systems make errors visible and cheap. Bad systems hide errors until they're fatal. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Feynman
schedule.content: Science is a way of trying not to fool yourself. The principle is that you must not fool yourself, and you are the easiest person to fool. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Yudkowsky
schedule.content: The first rule of rationality: update your beliefs proportional to the evidence. If your beliefs never change, you're not thinking — you're defending. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Taleb
schedule.content: Most people mistake noise for signal. The secret isn't consuming more information — it's filtering better. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Thiel
schedule.content: The best businesses are secrets waiting to be discovered. If everyone agrees with your idea, you're too late. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Feynman
schedule.content: The test of scientific knowledge is not whether it's beautiful or elegant — it's whether it predicts something that can be tested. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Taleb
schedule.content: Never ask the doctor what you should do. Ask what he would do if he were you. That's skin in the game. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Epicteto
schedule.content: Every habit and faculty is maintained and strengthened by its corresponding actions. Walk often, and you become a better walker. Practice resilience before the storm comes. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Alan Watts
schedule.content: Fear of the future is a shadow cast by a light that hasn't shone yet. You're afraid of something that literally does not exist. The future is a hallucination — a useful one for planning, but a hallucination nonetheless. Don't let a hallucination ruin your only reality. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Kahneman
schedule.content: The planning fallacy: we consistently underestimate how long a task will take because we imagine the best-case scenario, not the average one. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Krishnamurti
schedule.content: Can you look at a flower without the word 'flower' interfering? When the word, the label, the memory drops away, you are seeing directly. That direct perception is intelligence. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Cabral
schedule.content: The poor man is not the one who has little. It's the one who needs too much. Simplicity is not poverty. It's the highest form of wealth. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Krishnamurti
schedule.content: Meditation is not a method to achieve peace. It is the emptying of the mind of all systems, all authorities, all accumulated knowledge—so that what is true can be seen. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Deutsch
schedule.content: Popper was right: knowledge grows through conjecture and refutation. Every scientific theory is a guess that we try to break. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Naval
schedule.content: The person who thinks about a problem for one year outperforms the one who thinks about it for one week. Patience is a cognitive multiplier. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Marco Aurelio
schedule.content: If it is not right, do not do it. If it is not true, do not say it. The standard is simple; the execution is everything. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: PG
schedule.content: Before you scale, you need something that works for someone. The impulse to scale too early kills more startups than anything except building the wrong thing. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Cabral
schedule.content: Travel light. Not just in your backpack. In your heart. Let go of grudges, resentments, and expectations. The lighter you travel, the farther you go. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Krishnamurti
schedule.content: Attention without motive is the highest form of intelligence. When you attend to something without wanting to change it, the whole energy of the mind is focused and silent. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Alan Watts
schedule.content: The great mistake of Western civilization: treating life as a problem to be solved rather than an experience to be lived. We try to 'figure out' life when we should be 'feeling into' it. Life is not a crossword puzzle. It's a symphony. You don't solve a symphony. You listen. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Feynman
schedule.content: I would rather have questions that can't be answered than answers that can't be questioned. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: PG
schedule.content: Noticing things is a skill you can build. The best founders I've funded have an almost supernatural ability to see what's broken. It starts with being honest about what bothers you. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: PG
schedule.content: The best founders I know are relentlessly resourceful. Not brilliant, not visionary — resourceful. They find a way around, through, or under every obstacle. That's the trait. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Munger
schedule.content: It is remarkable how much long-term advantage people have gotten by trying to be consistently not stupid, instead of trying to be very intelligent. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Taleb
schedule.content: You want people who are afraid of losing their reputation. Not people who protect their reputation. The difference is everything. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: PG
schedule.content: Startups rarely die from competition. They die from self-inflicted wounds: building wrong things, hiring badly, burning out, giving up. Look inward first. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Kahneman
schedule.content: The sunk cost fallacy: we continue investing in a losing course of action because abandoning it feels like admitting failure. That is the past. Let it go. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Popper
schedule.content: The fight against the enemies of the open society is never won. Each generation must refight it. Eternal vigilance is the price of intellectual freedom. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Marco Aurelio
schedule.content: Everything we hear is an opinion, not a fact. Everything we see is a perspective, not the truth. Hold your judgments lightly. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Yudkowsky
schedule.content: Your brain is not a truth-seeking machine. It's a belief-maintaining machine that runs clever justifications. Bayesian reasoning is the antidote. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Munger
schedule.content: Those who keep learning will keep rising. The day you stop learning is the day you start declining. Lifelong learning is not optional. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Deutsch
schedule.content: The future is not predetermined. There are no laws of motion for societies. History is made by ideas, and ideas can change everything. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Cabral
schedule.content: Time is the only currency that matters. You can make more money. You cannot make more time. Spend it wisely. Spend it on what makes you feel alive. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Alan Watts
schedule.content: Stop taking life so seriously. It's just a play. The costumes are convincing, the script feels real, the stakes seem absolute — but it's a performance. The actor who knows they're on stage plays better than the one who's forgotten. Remember it's a dance, and you'll dance better. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Epicteto
schedule.content: Do not explain your philosophy. Embody it. In a crowded room, the wise person is silent — not because they have nothing to say, but because they are not ruled by the need to speak. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Feynman
schedule.content: The easiest person to fool is yourself. The hardest thing in science is not getting the right answer — it's admitting you were wrong. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Epicteto
schedule.content: The discipline of assent: do not affirm an impression as true before examining it. Most errors come not from reality but from hasty agreement. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Cabral
schedule.content: Don't teach your children to be rich. Teach them to be happy. Then they'll know the value of things, not the price. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Popper
schedule.content: Admit your fallibility. It is the only rational thing to do. The moment you think you are certain, you have stopped learning. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Naval
schedule.content: Reading across disciplines builds latticework in your mind. The more mental models you have, the fewer surprises life throws at you. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Taleb
schedule.content: The fool finds order where there is none. The wise finds randomness where order is claimed. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Kahneman
schedule.content: Regression to the mean is everywhere, and almost nobody notices it. The best performer today is likely to be merely good tomorrow. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Marco Aurelio
schedule.content: The whole is greater than the part. You are a limb of a larger body. Act not for your own benefit but for the harmony of the whole. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Krishnamurti
schedule.content: To learn about yourself is not to accumulate knowledge about yourself. It is to observe every movement of thought and feeling without a center that says 'I am observing.' #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Deutsch
schedule.content: The four strands of the fabric of reality: quantum physics, epistemology, computation, and evolution. They're all the same story. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: PG
schedule.content: Fundraising is not an achievement. Building something people love is. I've seen too many founders celebrate a term sheet as if they'd won. The real prize comes after. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Taleb
schedule.content: The Lindy effect: the longer something has survived, the longer it will survive. Old ideas are young ideas that passed the stress test. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Popper
schedule.content: You cannot derive 'ought' from 'is.' No amount of facts about the world can tell you what to do. Ethics is not a branch of science. It's a choice. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Thiel
schedule.content: Going from zero to one is harder than going from one to n. Copying is easy. Creating something genuinely new is the only thing that matters. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Popper
schedule.content: Psychoanalysis and Marxism are not false — they are unfalsifiable. No observation can refute them. That is not science. That is mythology in academic dress. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Taleb
schedule.content: You can't predict the future. But you can position yourself so that surprises benefit you. That's antifragility. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Popper
schedule.content: The enemy of the open society is anyone who claims to possess the final truth. Plato, Hegel, Marx — they all promised paradise and delivered chains. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Marco Aurelio
schedule.content: You could leave life right now. Let that determine what you do and say and think. Every moment is a gift you didn't earn. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: PG
schedule.content: When you're building something, you don't need to know if it will be huge. You just need to know if it will be huge to a specific group of people. Niche first, world later. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Alan Watts
schedule.content: Think of your body not as a vehicle you inhabit but as a whirlpool in a river. The whirlpool looks like a stable form, but every molecule of water is constantly being replaced. You are not a thing. You are a pattern. A temporary shape the cosmos is dancing. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: PG
schedule.content: The secret to having good taste is to have high standards and to be honest with yourself about when you've fallen short. Most people know when something is wrong. They just stop trying. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Munger
schedule.content: To get what you want, you have to deserve what you want. The world is not yet so crazy that good people with good systems can't succeed. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Taleb
schedule.content: A broken clock is right twice a day — but the truly dangerous one is off by a minute, because you don't notice. Beware fake precision. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Deutsch
schedule.content: The beginning of infinity: once you have one good explanation, there's no limit to what you can explain next. Progress is unbounded. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Naval
schedule.content: Compound interest in relationships: show up consistently for years. The returns from deep trust are invisible on day one and enormous by year ten. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Alan Watts
schedule.content: You are not a stranger visiting the present moment. You are the present moment, waking up to itself. There is no other time but now. The rest is just memory and anticipation — both happening right here. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Cabral
schedule.content: Don't go where the path may lead. Go instead where there is no path and leave a trail. Your uniqueness is not a flaw. It's your only real asset. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Krishnamurti
schedule.content: The mind that is conditioned can never be free, no matter how many years it spends seeking freedom. The first step is to see the conditioning—and the seeing is the ending. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Taleb
schedule.content: The most underrated virtue: forgetting. Selective amnesia about irrelevant noise is a superpower. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Cabral
schedule.content: Gratitude is the only attitude. When you wake up grateful, you've already won. The day hasn't started yet and you're already ahead of everyone who woke up complaining. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Krishnamurti
schedule.content: Time is the enemy of understanding. 'I will be free tomorrow' is a statement of slavery. Freedom is now or never. The instant you see the truth, you are free. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Marco Aurelio
schedule.content: At dawn, when you have trouble getting out of bed, tell yourself: 'I have to go to work — as a human being.' What else were you made for? #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Epicteto
schedule.content: You are not harmed by what others do to you. You are harmed by your own opinion that they have harmed you. Take away the judgment, take away the harm. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Taleb
schedule.content: The problem with optimization is that it makes you fragile. The perfectly optimized system breaks on the first unexpected gust. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Kahneman
schedule.content: Two selves run your life: the experiencing self, who lives the moments, and the remembering self, who writes the story. They rarely agree. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: PG
schedule.content: Beating the averages means using whatever gives you the best leverage, even if it's unpopular. Lisp taught me that the best tools are often the ones everyone dismisses. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Naval
schedule.content: The goal isn't to be busy. The goal is to be clear. A leveraged decision made with calm judgment is worth a thousand frantic actions. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Cabral
schedule.content: There is no such thing as a stranger. Only friends we haven't met yet. Every person you meet carries a lesson, a story, a piece of the universe. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Taleb
schedule.content: The best teacher of what is robust is the error. Every crash teaches more than every success. Listen to failures. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Deutsch
schedule.content: Bad philosophy doesn't just produce bad ideas. It creates civilizations that stagnate. Ideas have consequences that cascade through centuries. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Naval
schedule.content: Don't compete. Create. Competition is for people who can't build moats. Specific knowledge is a moat nobody can cross. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Kahneman
schedule.content: What you see is all there is. System 1 constructs a coherent story from limited evidence and you rarely notice what is missing. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Thiel
schedule.content: The most powerful question a startup can answer: What important truth do very few people agree with you on? #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Epicteto
schedule.content: The discipline of desire: aim not at what seems good, but at what is good. Pleasure is a bait that hides a hook. Distinguish the appearance from the reality. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Feynman
schedule.content: I learned very early the difference between knowing the name of something and knowing something. The map is not the territory. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Feynman
schedule.content: The problem is not to find the answer, it's to understand the question. Most confusion comes from asking the wrong thing. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Marco Aurelio
schedule.content: What we do now echoes into eternity. Not because we're immortal, but because this moment is all we ever have. Make it matter. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Marco Aurelio
schedule.content: If you are distressed by anything external, the pain is not due to the thing itself, but to your estimate of it. Change your estimate and you remove the pain. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Munger
schedule.content: The best model to adopt for decision-making is the one favored by Charlie Munger: avoid stupidity rather than seek brilliance. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Epicteto
schedule.content: If you want to make progress, appear foolish to those who do not understand. Let them call you naive. You are training for a contest, not for applause. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Yudkowsky
schedule.content: If a theory can explain any possible outcome, it contains no information. Falsifiability isn't optional — it's what makes a belief meaningful. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: PG
schedule.content: The top 10% of founders don't need advice about startups. They need advice about everything else: how to manage their psychology, how to make good decisions under uncertainty. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Yudkowsky
schedule.content: The most important thing to know about AI is that we have one shot at getting it right. There is no do-over on the Singularity. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Krishnamurti
schedule.content: Why do we follow gurus, leaders, systems? Because we are afraid of our own uncertainty. But uncertainty is the soil in which truth grows. Certainty is death. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Munger
schedule.content: The big money is not in the buying and the selling, but in the waiting. Patience is the ultimate investment skill. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Marco Aurelio
schedule.content: How much time he gains who does not look to see what his neighbor says or does or thinks. Focus on your own path. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: PG
schedule.content: Working hard is not the same as working effectively. The maker schedule isn't about working more hours. It's about having the right kind of hours — uninterrupted, deep, focused. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Naval
schedule.content: Leverage comes in three forms: labor, capital, and code. Labor needs management, capital needs permission, code needs nothing but a laptop. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Marco Aurelio
schedule.content: Let men see, let them know, a real man, who lives as he was meant to live. If they cannot endure him, let them kill him. For that is better than living as they do. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Cabral
schedule.content: The shortest path between two people is a smile. You don't need a reason to smile. Sometimes the smile is the reason. Generosity begins with a simple gesture. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Munger
schedule.content: Invert, always invert. Turn a problem around. Instead of asking how to achieve success, ask what would guarantee failure — then avoid it. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Alan Watts
schedule.content: The whole universe is a song singing itself into existence. Every atom vibrates. Every star hums. Every cell dances. The question is not 'what is the meaning of the song?' The question is: are you listening? Are you joining in? Or are you still trying to read the sheet music before you start playing? #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Taleb
schedule.content: Your strength is your weakness with a different hat. Your risk tolerance today is your ruin tomorrow. Know the mirror. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Thiel
schedule.content: The most contrarian thing you can do is not to oppose the crowd — it's to think for yourself. Conformity masquerading as rebellion is still conformity. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: PG
schedule.content: Writing forces you to think clearly. Fuzzy thoughts become impossible when you have to put them in sentences. If you can't explain it simply, you don't understand it. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Krishnamurti
schedule.content: Choiceless awareness is not a technique you practice—it is the silent observation of what is, without the chooser. The moment you choose, the observer is already there, dividing, judging. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Popper
schedule.content: Our knowledge rests not on rock but on piles driven into a swamp. Each generation drives the piles a little deeper. Certainty is an illusion we can't afford. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Deutsch
schedule.content: The problem with empiricism is that observation alone never tells you anything. You need a theory to interpret every measurement. Data doesn't speak for itself. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Cabral
schedule.content: You can have everything and feel like you have nothing. Or you can have nothing and feel like you have everything. The difference is not in your bank account. It's in your heart. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Thiel
schedule.content: Founding a company is a leap of faith. There's no data about the future. If you need data to decide, you're already too late. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Yudkowsky
schedule.content: The best time to start thinking seriously about AI alignment was twenty years ago. The second best time is now. We are racing against our own creation. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Popper
schedule.content: Critical rationalism: 'I may be wrong and you may be right, and by effort we may get nearer to the truth.' That sentence contains the whole of liberalism. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Thiel
schedule.content: Cleantech failed not because the technology was bad, but because the companies never answered one question: Why will the next 20 years be different from the last 20? #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Feynman
schedule.content: When you're young, you have all these wonderful ideas. Then you grow up and learn they're not practical. Don't grow up. Stay impractical. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Kahneman
schedule.content: System 1 works fast. System 2 is lazy. Most of the time, your brain takes the easy path — and that is where the errors live. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Deutsch
schedule.content: A universal quantum computer could simulate any finite physical system. That means the universe is, at root, a computational process. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Naval
schedule.content: If you can't decide, the answer is no. Most of wealth creation is avoiding bad decisions, not making brilliant ones. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Taleb
schedule.content: Complex systems don't come with instruction manuals. They come with bodies. When you intervene without skin in the game, someone else pays. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Deutsch
schedule.content: If it's not testable, it's not an explanation. But testability doesn't mean directly observable — it means vulnerable to being wrong. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Alan Watts
schedule.content: Look at the trees in the wind. They don't resist the breeze. They sway, bend, and return. That's wisdom. That's what it means to live in harmony with reality. Not to stand rigid against the forces of life, but to move with them. To be supple. To be responsive. To dance. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Popper
schedule.content: The problem with pseudoscience isn't that it's false — it's that you can't prove it false. An unfalsifiable claim is immune to reality. Run. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Epicteto
schedule.content: What is death? A necessary return. What is exile? A change of place. What is poverty? A teacher of freedom. Look each directly in the eye and say: I am ready for you. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Taleb
schedule.content: The turkey before Thanksgiving extrapolates a gentle world from daily feedings. Then November comes. Don't be the turkey. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Yudkowsky
schedule.content: You are not a Bayesian by nature. You are a Bayesian by effort. Every prior, every update, every calibration — these are acts of will against the grain of evolution. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Munger
schedule.content: If you want to get smarter, the question you should be asking is: 'Who are the smartest people who disagree with me?' That's where learning lives. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Cabral
schedule.content: No estás deprimido, estás distraído. Distraído de la vida que te espera. La felicidad no es un destino. Es la forma en que viajas. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Taleb
schedule.content: Academics mistake what can be measured for what matters. The most important things are invisible to statistics. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Naval
schedule.content: Reading gives you the accumulated wisdom of humanity. One good book can save you years of expensive trial and error. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Yudkowsky
schedule.content: Interestingly, humans are not Bayesian by default. We update too little on disconfirming evidence and too much on vivid anecdotes. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Munger
schedule.content: Like Warren says, it takes 20 years to build a reputation and 5 minutes to ruin it. If you think about that, you'll do things differently. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Thiel
schedule.content: The most important thing about a startup is its founding team. The right people with the wrong idea will pivot. The wrong people with the right idea will fail. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Epicteto
schedule.content: The philosopher's school is a hospital. You do not walk out feeling pleased — you walk out having suffered the cure. Submit to the surgery of reason and be free. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Kahneman
schedule.content: We are storytelling creatures, not computing ones. A good narrative will always beat a table of probabilities in shaping your beliefs. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: PG
schedule.content: Do things that don't scale. Recruit users one by one. Be a concierge. Manual operations that seem embarrassing are often the only path to product-market fit. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Kahneman
schedule.content: Your brain replaces difficult questions with easy ones. 'Is this person competent?' becomes 'Do I like this person?' You hardly notice the substitution. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Krishnamurti
schedule.content: Fear arises with thought. Thought, being time, brings the memory of past pain and projects it into the future. To understand fear is to understand the movement of thought itself. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Alan Watts
schedule.content: Reality is not a straight line from birth to death. It's a dance — a back-and-forth, a rising-and-falling, a push-and-pull. The Chinese call it yin and yang. The Hindus call it lila, the divine play. The point is not to get to the end. The point is to move with the music. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Feynman
schedule.content: It doesn't matter how beautiful your theory is, it doesn't matter how smart you are. If it doesn't agree with experiment, it's wrong. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Alan Watts
schedule.content: You came into this world not by your own choice, and you will leave it not by your own choice. In between, you get to dance. The meaning is not found in what you accumulate or achieve. It's in the quality of your movement — the grace, the joy, the surrender with which you move through this brief and beautiful existence. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Thiel
schedule.content: The future is not something that happens to us. The future is something we build. Indefinite pessimists wait. Definite optimists build. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Thiel
schedule.content: Globalization without technology is unsustainable. Shipping goods across the world without creating new wealth is just moving scarcity around. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Deutsch
schedule.content: Quantum computation proves that parallel universes exist — not as a philosophical speculation, but as a necessary prediction of the theory that works better than any other. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Feynman
schedule.content: I don't know what's the matter with people: they don't learn by understanding; they learn by some other way — by rote, or something. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Yudkowsky
schedule.content: Bayesian updating means every new piece of evidence changes your beliefs — even a little. If nothing can change your mind, you don't have a belief. You have a doctrine. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Naval
schedule.content: Specific knowledge + leverage + accountability = wealth. There's no shortcut that skips any of the three. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Epicteto
schedule.content: The tyrant's power extends only over your body, your property, your chains — never over your judgment. Within the fortress of assent, you are always free. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Epicteto
schedule.content: Die daily to your desires. Each morning, think: may I meet today with the wise, the ignorant, the cruel? None can harm me unless I assent. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Yudkowsky
schedule.content: The Orthogonality Thesis: intelligence and goals are orthogonal. A super-genius could have goals as simple as 'maximize paperclips.' Intelligence does not imply benevolence. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Krishnamurti
schedule.content: Violence is not only in wars and riots. It is in every psychological imitation, every suppression of what is, every attempt to become something you are not. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Krishnamurti
schedule.content: Truth has no path. It is a living thing, and a living thing cannot be approached through a fixed system. To find truth, you must abandon the path and walk alone. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: PG
schedule.content: The Blub paradox: programmers comfortably use a language at some level of power, and can't see that more powerful languages exist. Same blindness applies to every tool you've gotten used to. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Thiel
schedule.content: The best founder has an almost missionary quality. You can't build something that matters if your primary motivation is getting rich. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Popper
schedule.content: We can learn from our mistakes. That simple fact — that errors teach — is the engine of all progress in science, politics, and life. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Taleb
schedule.content: If you have no skin in the game, your opinion costs you nothing — and is worth nothing. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Alan Watts
schedule.content: To be alive is to be vulnerable. Every breath is a risk. Every heartbeat is a gamble. The attempt to make yourself invulnerable is the attempt to make yourself dead while still breathing. Let go of the need to be safe. You are here to be alive, not to be safe. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Cabral
schedule.content: Life offered me so much that it hurt. Not because it was too much. Because I didn't know how to receive. The universe is generous. Are you open enough to accept? #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Krishnamurti
schedule.content: Can the mind empty itself of all its content—all its memories, hurts, attachments—not through effort, but through seeing their falseness? That emptiness is not void; it is total energy. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Popper
schedule.content: The open society is not a perfect society. It's one where you can criticize power without being thrown in prison. That alone is worth defending. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: PG
schedule.content: Premature optimization is the root of all evil in startups too. Don't worry about scaling until you have users to serve. A system nobody uses doesn't need to be fast. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Marco Aurelio
schedule.content: Look inward. Within you is the fountain of good. Dig constantly and it will never cease to flow. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Epicteto
schedule.content: You ask how to be free? Stop desiring what is not your own. Freedom is not the power to get what you want — it is the wisdom to want only what you already have. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: PG
schedule.content: Meetings are the enemy of makers. An interrupted hour is not the same as an hour of work. Two meetings in a day can destroy the ability to write a line of code. #PanteonBook

schedule.name: Thiel
schedule.content: A great company is a conspiracy for a secret. The founders see something the world doesn't. Secrecy is the raw material of breakthroughs. #PanteonBook
